mission.title: Migrate persistent application data to a new storage class
mission.description: Operational mission for safely moving Kubernetes workloads to new persistent volumes by snapshotting data, recreating claims, and validating application state.

steps.title: Identify the PVCs and workloads that need migration
steps.description:
Capture the namespace, current storage class, access mode, and the workloads mounting the PVC before making changes.
bash
NAMESPACE=app-prod
kubectl get pvc -n "$NAMESPACE" -o wide
kubectl get pods -n "$NAMESPACE" -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\t"}{range .spec.volumes[*]}{.persistentVolumeClaim.claimName}{" "}{end}{"\n"}{end}'

Confirm the target CSI driver supports VolumeSnapshot and provisioning from snapshot before continuing.

steps.title: Create a VolumeSnapshot of the source PVC
steps.description:
Quiesce the workload if the application requires crash-consistent snapshots, then create a VolumeSnapshot using the correct snapshot class.
bash
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -n "$NAMESPACE" -f -
apiVersion: snapshot.storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: VolumeSnapshot
metadata:
  name: app-data-migration-snap
spec:
  volumeSnapshotClassName: csi-snapclass
  source:
    persistentVolumeClaimName: app-data
EOF
kubectl get volumesnapshot -n "$NAMESPACE" app-data-migration-snap -o yaml

Wait until status.readyToUse: true before creating the replacement claim.
commands:
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -n "$NAMESPACE" -f -
apiVersion: snapshot.storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: VolumeSnapshot
metadata:
  name: app-data-migration-snap
spec:
  volumeSnapshotClassName: csi-snapclass
  source:
    persistentVolumeClaimName: app-data
EOF

steps.title: Create a new PVC from the snapshot
steps.description:
Provision a destination claim on the new storage class using the snapshot as the data source.
bash
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -n "$NAMESPACE" -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: app-data-migrated
spec:
  accessModes:
  - ReadWriteOnce
  storageClassName: fast-ssd
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 100Gi
  dataSource:
    name: app-data-migration-snap
    kind: VolumeSnapshot
    apiGroup: snapshot.storage.k8s.io
EOF
kubectl get pvc -n "$NAMESPACE" app-data-migrated

Match size and access mode requirements from the original claim unless the target driver requires a larger allocation.
commands:
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -n "$NAMESPACE" -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: app-data-migrated
spec:
  accessModes:
  - ReadWriteOnce
  storageClassName: fast-ssd
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 100Gi
  dataSource:
    name: app-data-migration-snap
    kind: VolumeSnapshot
    apiGroup: snapshot.storage.k8s.io
EOF

steps.title: Update the workload to use the new PVC
steps.description:
Patch the Deployment, StatefulSet, or Helm values so new Pods mount the migrated claim, then roll the workload.
bash
kubectl set volume deployment/my-app -n "$NAMESPACE" \
  --add --name=app-data --claim-name=app-data-migrated --mount-path=/var/lib/app --overwrite
kubectl rollout restart deployment/my-app -n "$NAMESPACE"
kubectl rollout status deployment/my-app -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=5m

For StatefulSets or Helm-managed apps, make the equivalent declarative change in the workload manifest and apply it.

steps.title: Verify data integrity on the new volume
steps.description:
Confirm the application starts cleanly, mounted data is present, and a sample checksum or directory listing matches expectations.
bash
POD=$(kubectl get pods -n "$NAMESPACE" -l app=my-app -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}')
kubectl exec -n "$NAMESPACE" "$POD" -- df -h /var/lib/app
kubectl exec -n "$NAMESPACE" "$POD" -- ls -lah /var/lib/app | head

Run any application-specific smoke tests now; this is the best point to detect truncated or inconsistent restored data.

steps.title: Clean up the old PVC after validation
steps.description:
Once the application has run successfully on the new volume and rollback is no longer needed, remove the old claim and snapshot objects.
bash
kubectl scale deployment/my-app -n "$NAMESPACE" --replicas=0
kubectl delete pvc -n "$NAMESPACE" app-data
kubectl delete volumesnapshot -n "$NAMESPACE" app-data-migration-snap
kubectl scale deployment/my-app -n "$NAMESPACE" --replicas=1

If you need a rollback window, keep the old claim detached for an agreed retention period instead of deleting it immediately.

resolution.summary: The workload now runs on a new PVC created from a verified snapshot, and the original storage resources can be retired after validation.
